one_time_description: Loss on assumption of debt from K'ul Management Group Ltd. including assumption of debt $4,710,461, forgiveness of debt $1,050,423, partially offset by gain on transfer of work in progress assets $245,896
economic_notes: Band owns 100% of K'ul Management Group Ltd. which holds controlling investments in Westhills Aggregates LP, Coyote Cruises LP, Snpinktn Forestry LP, K'ul Construction LP, Skaha Hills Residential Development LP, and K'ul Management Group LP. Band holds 20% interest in Skaha Hills Limited Partnership and 25% interest in SnPink'Tn Developments Limited Partnership. Property tax administration system in place. Band holds Revenue and Capital Trusts from own source revenues. Defined contribution pension plan administered through Capri Insurance/Great West Life.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (ended March 31, 2022). Consolidated entity includes PIB government, Penticton Indian Band Utilities Limited Partnership (PIBULP), and Okanagan Nation Family Intervention and Services Society (ONTEH). Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: K'ul Management Group Ltd. (100% owned), Skaha Hills Limited Partnership (20%), and SnPink'Tn Developments Limited Partnership (25%). Large one-time loss on assumption of debt of $5,514,989 in Economic Development segment related to transactions with K'ul Management Group Ltd. Comparative figures restated due to two corrections: $112,763 revenue incorrectly included in prior year and $464,441 revenue incorrectly excluded in prior year. Net income from business enterprise (KMGLTD) was $5,921,978 and net income from business partnerships was $303,068, both included in annual surplus but not in total revenues/expenditures line. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $12,900,236 + CMHC $274,448 + FNHA $1,637,155 + FNESC $1,707,027 + OTDC $112,323 + Province of BC $2,313,329 + BC First Nations Gaming $302,247 - OSR includes housing charges, interest, other contracts, property taxes, royalties. Expenses_salaries_total from segmented schedule wages and benefits $9,461,928 differs slightly from expenditures by object salaries $9,027,718 due to segment allocation differences. Annual debt service is principal repayments only per cash flow statement.
